title: Responds quickly to --help
rationale: Startup time is part of a CLI's UX; help in particular should feel instant. Defer heavy work until the invoked command needs it.
remediation: Avoid loading heavy dependencies before parsing arguments; keep --help fast.

title: Does not hang when run with no arguments
rationale: A CLI that silently blocks on stdin when arguments are missing looks frozen to users and stalls scripts and agents.
remediation: When required input is missing, print help or an error and exit; do not block on a TTY read.
